コンコン――: There was a knock at the door.
お前が今日着任した志願兵か。: So you're the volunteer who arrived today.
はいっ！ <br>ミレスガルド出身のベティであります！: Yes! I am Betty of Milesgard!
はは、元気なやつだな。<br>前線基地への配属を希望しているみたいだが、どうして:
Haha, you're a lively one. I heard you requested assignment to the
Frontline Base—why's that?
はい！ わたしの実家は代々騎士の家柄で<br>幼いころより父や兄の戦場での活躍を:
Yes! My family has been knights for generations. Since childhood, I've
witnessed my father and brother's heroic deeds on the battlefield!
だからわたしも父や兄のような<br>立派な兵士になって活躍するのが目標なのであり:
That's why my goal is to become a great soldier like my father and
brother, and to make a name for myself!
なるほど。家族にもさぞかし期待されているんだろうな。: I see. Your family must be expecting great things from you.
実は家族からは、兵士になることを反対されておりまして……: Actually, my family has opposed my becoming a soldier...
わたしのような小柄な女性に<br>危険な戦場の任務など務まるはずはないと……:
They said that a small woman like me could never handle dangerous
battlefield duties...
……なるほどな。だったらここに来たのは正解だな。: ...I see. Then you came to the right place.
ここは戦場の最前線。手柄を取るチャンスはそこかしこに転がっている。<br>ここで:
This is the front line. Chances to prove yourself are everywhere. Earn
merits here and show your family you've got what it takes.
はっ、はいっ！ きっと司令官殿のご期待に応えてみせるであります！<br>それでは:
Yes, sir! I will surely meet your expectations, Commander! Now, I shall
take my leave!
とても元気で、いい子ですね。: She is quite spirited, and seems like a fine girl.
ああ。ああいうやつがいると、場の空気がよくなる。: Yeah. Having someone like that around really lifts the mood.
……というわけで、大穴内の掘削作業は現在、難航しております。<br>専門技術を持:
So, excavation inside the Abyss is proving difficult. We had high
hopes for the Engineer Corps with their specialized skills, but...
蓋を開けてみれば、誰が隊長として現場を取り仕切るかで揉めてばかりで<br>仕事が:
When we actually looked into it, they've been fighting endlessly over
who should take charge as captain, and work has hardly progressed.
……認めた相手の言うことしか聞かないってところか。<br>職人気質な連中ってのは:
So they'll only listen to someone they acknowledge. These
craftsman-types can be a real handful at times like this.
隊長か……いっそ、俺が指名してしまうか？<br>いや、頭ごなしの行動をとればやつ:
The captain, huh... Should I just appoint one myself? No, if I take a
high-handed approach, they'll only resist more...
……考えてもらちが明かないな。<br>ちょっと外の空気を吸ってくる。:
Thinking about it isn't getting me anywhere. I'm going out to get some
fresh air.
荒くれ者揃いの工兵隊のまとめ役、か……<br>どうしたものか…… ん？: A leader for that rowdy bunch of engineers, huh... What to do... Hmm?
まさか司令官殿に名前を憶えていてもらえたなんて……！<br>感激であります！:
To think you remembered my name, Lord Commander... I'm deeply
honored!
大げさだな。<br>それはそうと……なにかあったのか？: You're exaggerating. But anyway... Did something happen?
浮かない顔でため息ついてたろ？: You were sighing with a gloomy face, weren't you?
……はは。バレちゃいましたか。<br>さすがは司令官殿であります。: ...Haha, you caught me. That's the Lord Commander for you.
実は……モンスターを討伐する部隊に配属されたでありますが<br>洞窟の中で動けな:
Actually... I was assigned to a monster-hunting squad, but I froze up
in a cave and caused trouble for my comrades.
それが、そのぅ……わたし、暗い場所が苦手でして。<br>怖くて動けなくなってしま:
Well, um... I'm not good with dark places. I got scared and couldn't
move.
それは難儀だな……<br>だったら配置転換を願い出ればどうだ？: That's tough... If so, why not request a transfer?
そう思って、主計兵に配置換えしてもらったであります。<br>消耗品の管理や調理を:
That's why I had myself reassigned as a quartermaster. I thought that
as a quartermaster handling supplies and cooking, I could be of use.
いえ、料理はむしろ得意であります。<br>ですが……: No, cooking is actually my strong suit. But...
コース料理を振る舞ってしまい<br>戦場でこんなもの呑気に食べてられるか！ と怒:
I served a full-course meal, and they got angry, saying, 'Who's got
the leisure to eat something like this on the battlefield!'
おいしい食事で元気になってもらおうと思ったのが<br>仇となったであります。: I thought a tasty meal would cheer them up, but it backfired.
そんなことが度々あった末に、ついには兵士失格だと言われ、<br>仕事を任せてもら:
After a string of such failures, I was eventually told I was a failure as
a soldier, and they stopped giving me any tasks...
こんなダメダメ兵士、手柄を立てるなんて夢のまた夢であります。<br>故郷の両親に:
A useless soldier like me earning merits? A pipe dream. How can I
write home? *sigh*...
ベティ。さっきから何をしているんだ？: Betty, what have you been doing?
ああ、すみません、つい癖で……。<br>これを磨いていたであります。: Ah, sorry, it's a habit... I was polishing this.
これは……石か？<br>なんでそんなことを。: This... is a stone? Why would you do that?
ん？ その石、随分と光ってないか？: Huh? That stone, it's shining awfully bright, isn't it?
長年、土に埋もれて一見、くすんだ石ころも<br>丁寧に根気よく磨いてあげると宝石:
Even a dull pebble buried in the earth for years can shine like a
beautiful gem if you polish it carefully and patiently!
石と言えば、大穴にはきっと、<br>地上では見られない色々な石があると思うのです:
Speaking of stones, I am sure there are all sorts of stones in the
Abyss that you cannot see on the surface!
ああ。大穴には一体どんな石があるんだろう……: Ah... I wonder just what kind of stones are in the Abyss...
……わわっ！？ す、すみません、１人ではしゃいでしまって！<br>司令官殿を前に:
Wah! I-I'm sorry, I got carried away all by myself! In front of the Lord
Commander, I ended up lost in my own world...
いや、構わないさ。<br>夢中になれるものがあるってのはいいことだ。: No, it's fine. Having something you can get so into is a good thing.
石のことなら何でも詳しいのか？: So you know all about stones, then?
もちろんであります！<br>ただこんな趣味、戦場では何の役にも立ちませんが……:
Of course! But a hobby like this is completely useless on the
battlefield...
って、くよくよなんかしていられませんよね！<br>明日から、また頑張らないと！:
I mean, I cannot be moping around, right! I have to work hard again
starting tomorrow!
もちろんであります！ 元気だけがわたしの取柄でありますから！<br>司令官殿も笑:
Of course! Enthusiasm is the only thing I have going for me! So you
too must give it your all with a smile, Lord Commander!
では、これで失礼します！: Well then, I shall take my leave!
ベティ、ちょっと俺に付き合ってくれないか？: Betty, would you come with me for a bit?
